message:
test(fcp-conformance/authority-view): pin AuthorityView::from_observed deterministic resolution (br-rybvy)

AuthorityView::from_observed is the deterministic routing-decision
resolver that takes observed leases and computes a coherent
(active_holder, active_fencing_token, coordinator, failover_order,
records, timeline) snapshot. Zero conformance coverage even though
this is what makes split-brain deterministically resolvable across
mesh peers.

Adds 10 tests against the public fcp_mesh::AuthorityView API:

- empty_observations_yield_no_active_holder
- higher_fencing_token_wins_among_active_leases
- tied_fencing_tokens_broken_by_later_expires_at
- tied_token_and_expiry_broken_lexicographically_by_holder
  (deterministic final tiebreaker — observation order MUST NOT
  determine the winner)
- expired_lease_with_higher_token_does_not_take_authority (active
  filter applied BEFORE token comparison; otherwise an expired-but-
  higher-token lease would steal authority)
- all_expired_leases_yield_no_active_holder
- winner_record_status_is_active_loser_is_superseded (per-record
  status tagging contract)
- expired_record_is_tagged_expired_regardless_of_token_rank
  (audit-trail preservation)
- from_observed_is_deterministic_under_observation_reordering (THE
  property that makes two peers with the same set of observations
  arrive at the same authority decision regardless of receive
  order)
- failover_order_covers_all_eligible_nodes (HRW ranking exhausts
  the eligible set; no drops, no extras)

All 10 pass locally in <0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
authority_view_resolution_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
